sanskrit:
यो मां पश्यति सर्वत्र सर्वं च मयि पश्यति।
तस्याहं न प्रणश्यामि स च मे न प्रणश्यति॥
transliteration:
yo māṃ paśyati sarvatra sarvaṃ ca mayi paśyati |
tasyāhaṃ na praṇaśyāmi sa ca me na praṇaśyati ||
literal_meaning: Whoever sees Me everywhere and sees everything in Me—of that person I am not lost, and that person is not lost to Me.
contextual_meaning: A mind that has internalized a stable, benevolent presence (symbolized by Kṛṣṇa) into every perceptual field experiences unbroken relational security; neither the self nor the internalized object can be psychologically “lost.”
psychological_state: Achieved object constancy—an internal working model in which the comforting presence is mentally accessible even when external cues are absent, preventing splitting or fragmentation.
trigger_scenario: A remote worker, living alone, feels persistently accompanied by an inner mentor or value system; setbacks do not trigger existential loneliness because the internalized support is co-conscious with every task.
anchor_text: See the same loving presence in every face and never feel alone again.
